questions.question: What is the time complexity of sequential searching?
questions.explanation: Sequential searching has a time complexity of O(n) as it checks each element in the list one by one.

questions.question: In sequential searching, what happens when the target element is not found in the list?
options: The algorithm continues searching from the beginning
The algorithm returns the element at the last index
questions.explanation: In sequential searching, when the target element is not found, the algorithm continues searching from the beginning of the list.

questions.question: What is the main difference between linear sequential searching and binary sequential searching?
options: Linear searching checks each element one by one while binary searching checks half of the list at a time
Linear searching checks half of the list at a time while binary searching checks each element one by one
Both linear and binary searching check each element one by one
Both linear and binary searching check half of the list at a time
questions.explanation: The main difference between linear sequential searching and binary sequential searching is that linear searching checks each element one by one, while binary searching checks half of the list at a time.

questions.question: In sequential searching, what is the advantage of using a sorted list?
options: The algorithm can handle duplicate elements
The algorithm can handle unsorted lists
questions.explanation: The main advantage of using a sorted list in sequential searching is that it can handle duplicate elements, as the algorithm can stop searching as soon as it finds the first occurrence of the target element.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of sequential searching on a sorted list?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of sequential searching on a sorted list is O(n) because the algorithm only needs to check each element once to find the target element.
